steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for an introduction to Ward’s career aimed at readers who already know MBC. That means the answer must: (1) name Ward as a notable figure within MBC, and (2) avoid re-explaining what MBC is or padding with irrelevant details. It should open the door to a discussion of Ward specifically.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — While Clarence Theodore Ward is considered a great composer, there are many more such composers included in MBC's Historic Composers Directory.: buries Ward’s significance by pivoting immediately to “many more,” which undercuts him as a subject. Shifts focus away from Ward rather than setting up his career. ✗
  • Choice B — Born in 1922, Clarence Theodore Ward passed away in 2018 according to the MBC project.: gives only biographical dates with no context about why Ward matters or what follows. Too thin to set up a career discussion. ✗
  • Choice C — Among the many talented figures included in the MBC project is classical music composer Clarence Theodore Ward.: situates Ward within MBC (which the reader already knows) and identifies him as a classical music composer, creating the right launching point for a career discussion. ✓
  • Choice D — The MBC project's Historic Composers Directory highlights Black classical composers of the past, such as Clarence Theodore Ward (1922–2018), in order to inspire music students to become the classical composers of the future.: re-explains MBC’s mission to an audience that already knows it. Uses Ward only as an example, not as the subject. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
